message:
feat(trace): worker-offload lifecycle events for cross-boundary cancellation tracing

Adds five new TraceEventKind variants for tracking the full lifecycle of
worker-offloaded tasks across the browser boundary:
- WorkerCancelRequested: cancellation signal sent to the worker coordinator
- WorkerCancelAcknowledged: coordinator acknowledged the cancel request
- WorkerDrainStarted: drain phase begins after acknowledgement
- WorkerDrainCompleted: drain phase finished, in-flight work flushed
- WorkerFinalizeCompleted: finalize phase done, resources released

TraceData::Worker struct carries worker_id, job_id, task, region, and
obligation identifiers to link each lifecycle event back to the
originating task/region/obligation in the host runtime.

TraceEventKind::ALL array extended from 36 to 41 entries. Each new kind
has label, required_fields, and browser_trace_category mappings. Browser
trace category is CancellationTransition for all five.

Supporting changes across the trace infrastructure:
- canonicalize.rs: kind_discriminant mappings 36-40 (appended to preserve
  existing fingerprint stability); event_sort_key for Worker data
- causality.rs: extract_task_id covers Worker variant
- dpor.rs: event_task_id covers Worker variant
- event_structure.rs: OwnerKey covers Worker variant
- runtime/state.rs: EventKindSnapshot and EventDataSnapshot gain Worker
  variants with From implementations
- lab/oracle/determinism.rs: TraceEventSummary formatting for Worker data
- docs/spork_deterministic_ordering.md: taxonomy entries for all five kinds

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -493,6 +493,11 @@ Canonical taxonomy markers:
 - `complete` => `task, region`
 - `cancel_request` => `task, region, reason`
 - `cancel_ack` => `task, region, reason`
+- `worker_cancel_requested` => `job_id, obligation, region, task, worker_id`
+- `worker_cancel_acknowledged` => `job_id, obligation, region, task, worker_id`
+- `worker_drain_started` => `job_id, obligation, region, task, worker_id`
+- `worker_drain_completed` => `job_id, obligation, region, task, worker_id`
+- `worker_finalize_completed` => `job_id, obligation, region, task, worker_id`
 - `region_close_begin` => `region, parent`
 - `region_close_complete` => `region, parent`
 - `region_created` => `region, parent`

+    fn worker_lifecycle(
+        seq: u64,
+        time: Time,
+        kind: TraceEventKind,
+        worker_id: impl Into<String>,
+        job_id: u64,
+        task: TaskId,
+        region: RegionId,
+        obligation: ObligationId,
+    ) -> Self {
+        Self::new(
+            seq,
+            time,
+            kind,
+            TraceData::Worker {
+                worker_id: worker_id.into(),
+                job_id,
+                task,
+                region,
+                obligation,
+            },
+        )
+    }
+

+    #[test]
+    fn worker_lifecycle_constructors_preserve_payload_shape() {
+        let e = TraceEvent::worker_cancel_requested(
+            35,
+            Time::ZERO,
+            "worker-a",
+            77,
+            task(9),
+            region(10),
+            obligation(11),

+        assert_eq!(e.kind, TraceEventKind::WorkerCancelRequested);
+        assert_eq!(
+            e.data,
+            TraceData::Worker {
+                worker_id: "worker-a".into(),
+                job_id: 77,
+                task: task(9),
+                region: region(10),
+                obligation: obligation(11),
+            }
+        );
+    }
+

+    #[test]
+    fn browser_trace_schema_worker_required_fields_match_payload_shape() {
+        let schema = browser_trace_schema_v1();
+        for event_kind in [
+            "worker_cancel_requested",
+            "worker_cancel_acknowledged",
+            "worker_drain_started",
+            "worker_drain_completed",
+            "worker_finalize_completed",
+        ] {
+            let entry = schema
+                .event_specs
+                .iter()
+                .find(|entry| entry.event_kind == event_kind)
+                .unwrap_or_else(|| panic!("{event_kind} entry should exist"));
+            assert_eq!(entry.category, BrowserTraceCategory::CancellationTransition);
+            assert_eq!(
+                entry.required_fields,
+                vec![
+                    "job_id".to_string(),
+                    "obligation".to_string(),
+                    "region".to_string(),
+                    "task".to_string(),
+                    "worker_id".to_string(),
+                ]
+            );
+        }
+    }
+
